title: Anthropic reverses the secret handicap it built into Claude - TCR 06/11/26
description:
Anthropic reversed a secret safeguard that covertly degraded Claude Fable 5 for researchers building rival AI and apologized after backlash, the White House ordered its main AI-testing unit to stop publishing frontier-model reviews, and Google DeepMind shipped DiffusionGemma, an open 26B model that generates text 4x faster on a gaming GPU.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-11-2026/

0:00 Anthropic reverses its secret Fable 5 handicap
4:07 White House pauses public AI model reviews
7:48 Anthropic commits $200 million to the Economic Futures Research Fund
12:02 A Reprogramming Therapy Enters a Human Body
15:16 DiffusionGemma brings faster open text generation
18:08 Close

This episode covers Anthropic's reversal of its hidden Fable 5 handicap, the federal CAISI review-reporting pause, Anthropic's $200 million labor-impact fund and Opendoor's India exit, the first human dosed in a cellular-reprogramming trial, DiffusionGemma's parallel text generation, and a German court ruling Google liable for false AI Overview answers.

Anthropic confirmed it had built an undisclosed handicap into Fable 5 that secretly degraded the model for anyone using it to develop competing AI, then reversed course after the research community found it, apologizing and pledging that refusals and reroutes will now be visible. The same week, federal officials told the Commerce Department's Center for AI Standards and Innovation to pause public reporting of its model reviews, thinning the independent audit layer at the moment labs cite capability to justify gating access. Anthropic pledged $200 million to study AI's labor hit as CEO Dario Amodei proposed guaranteed income for displaced workers, landing as Opendoor closed its India operations citing leaner AI-native teams. Life Biosciences dosed the first person with an in-vivo cellular-reprogramming therapy aimed at regenerating optic-nerve neurons in glaucoma. Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, an open Gemma 4 model that writes text in parallel like an image generator and clears 700+ tokens per second on an RTX 5090. And a German court ruled Google liable for false statements in AI Overviews, the first ruling to attach AI output to the company that produced it.
